title: Humidification chamber and apparatus and systems including or configured to include said chamber
abstract: In one embodiment, there is provided an inlet for a humidification chamber for use in a medical humidification system, the inlet comprising an inlet end configured to receive gases flow from a gases source, an outlet end configured to introduce the gases flow to the inside of the humidification chamber, and at least one wall defining, at least in part, a passageway between the inlet end and the outlet end for conveying gases therebetween. The outlet end of the inlet is configured to terminate in the humidification chamber or at a wall of the humidification chamber, and the passageway is configured to guide the gases flow such that the center of the gases flow at the inlet end and at the outlet end are substantially aligned along a common axis or the outlet end defines a profile of the passageway thereat that has a width and a height, a ratio of the width to height being between about 1:20 and 1:5.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An inlet for a humidification chamber for use in a medical humidification system, the inlet comprising an inlet end configured to receive gases flow from a gases source, an outlet end configured to introduce the gases flow to the inside of the humidification chamber, and at least one wall defining, at least in part, a passageway between the inlet end and the outlet end for conveying gases therebetween, wherein the outlet end of the inlet is configured to terminate in the humidification chamber or at a wall of the humidification chamber, and wherein the passageway is configured to guide the gases flow such that the center of the gases flow at the inlet end and at the outlet end are substantially aligned along a common axis. 2. The inlet of claim 1, configured such that at least a portion of the common axis is substantially tangential to a side wall of the humidification chamber. 3. The inlet of claim 2, configured such that said at least a portion of the common axis that is tangential to a side wall of the humidification chamber extends from or proximate the outlet end partly or completely towards the inlet end. 4. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein a cross sectional area of the passageway varies along at least a portion of the length thereof. 5. The inlet of claim 4, wherein the cross sectional area is smallest at and/or proximate to the outlet end. 6. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outlet end defines a profile of the passageway thereat that is an elongate shape. 7. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outlet end defines a profile of the passageway thereat that has a width to height ratio of between about 1 :20 and 1 :5. 8. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an inner surface of the at least one wall defining the passageway is configured to define a curved passageway along at least a portion of the length of the inlet. 9. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein an inner surface of the at least one wall defining the passageway is smooth and/or continuous. 10. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the outlet end is configured to be above a water level in the humidification chamber. 11. The inlet of claim 10, wherein the outlet end is configured to be at or proximate a top of the humidification chamber. 12. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, comprising a formation provided at or proximate to the outlet end, the formation being configured to prevent water in the humidification chamber entering the inlet. 13. The inlet of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the inlet is configured to releasably or permanently attach to the humidification chamber. 14. An inlet for a humidification chamber for use in a medical humidification system, the inlet comprising an inlet end configured to receive gases flow from a gases source, an outlet end configured to introduce the gases flow to the inside of the humidification chamber along an internal surface of a side wall of the humidification chamber, and at least one wall defining, at least in part, a passageway between the inlet end and the outlet end for conveying gases therebetween, wherein the outlet end defines a profile of the passageway thereat that has a width and a height, a ratio of the width to height being between about 1 :20 and 1 :5. 15. The inlet of claim 14, configured to introduce the gases flow to the humidification chamber at a direction substantially tangential to the side wall of the humidification chamber. 16. The inlet of claim 14 or 15, wherein the inlet end defines a first profile of the passageway and the outlet end defines a second profile of the passageway, wherein the first and second profiles are different in shape and/or size. 17. The inlet of claim 16, wherein the first profile is substantially rounded. 18. The inlet of claim 16, wherein the first profile is generally circular or oval. 19. The inlet of any one of claims 14 to 18, wherein an inner surface of the at least one wall defining the passageway is configured to define a curved passageway along at least a portion of the length of the inlet. 20. The inlet of any one of claims 14 to 19, wherein an inner surface of the at least one wall defining the passageway is smooth and/or continuous. 21. The inlet of any one of claims 14 to 20, wherein the outlet end is configured to be above a water level in the humidification chamber. 22. The inlet of claim 21, wherein the outlet end is configured to be at or proximate a top of the humidification chamber. 23. The inlet of any one of claims 14 to 22, comprising a formation provided at or proximate to the outlet end, the formation being configured to prevent water in the humidification chamber entering the inlet. 24. The inlet of any one of claims 14 to 23, wherein the inlet is configured to releasably or permanently attach to the humidification chamber. 25. A humidification chamber for use in a medical humidification system, the humidification chamber comprising: a base and a top linked by a side wall so as to define the chamber; the inlet of any one of claims 1 to 24, the inlet being configured to receive a gases flow from a gases source; and a gases outlet. 26. The humidification chamber of claim 25, wherein the inlet is, at least in part, integrally formed with the chamber. 27. The humidification chamber of claim 25 or 26, wherein the inlet is, at least in part, releasably or permanently attachable or attached to the humidification chamber. 28. The humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 27, wherein at least a portion of the inlet is parallel or angled obliquely towards the base of the humidification chamber and/or at least a portion of the inlet is perpendicular to or substantially perpendicular to the base of the humidification chamber. 29. The humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 28, wherein at least a portion of the gases outlet is perpendicular to or substantially perpendicular to or offset from perpendicular to the base of the humidification chamber. 30. The humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 29, wherein at least a portion of the gases outlet is perpendicular or substantially perpendicular or offset from perpendicular to the inlet. 31. The humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 30, wherein the gases outlet is disposed on the top of the humidification chamber. 32. A kit of parts for an unassembled respiratory therapy or other medical or surgical system, the kit comprising: a humidification chamber as defined in any one of claims 25 to 31 ; and any one or more of: a gases source; a supply conduit defining at least part of a gases flow path between the gases source and the humidification chamber; a patient conduit defining at least part of a gases flow path between the humidification chamber and a patient interface; a filter assembly; a patient interface; and a humidifier comprising a heater plate for heating the contents of the humidification chamber. 33. A respiratory therapy system, comprising a humidification apparatus and the humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 31. 34. A respiratory therapy system, comprising a humidification apparatus, a humidification chamber and the inlet of any one of claims 1 to24. 35. A medical insufflation system, comprising a humidification apparatus and the humidification chamber of any one of claims 25 to 31. 36. A medical insufflation system comprising a humidification apparatus, a humidification chamber and the inlet of any one of claims 1 to 24.
